stages.title: What happened in your run?
stages.intro: Start with what the site recorded. Then choose how the station felt.

stages.title: How safe was your approach?
stages.intro: Choose the closest match. Focus on urgent action and safe transport.

options.label: Likely safe
options.description: You called an ambulance promptly and kept David from driving.

options.label: Needs improvement
options.description: You recognised the danger, but the urgent plan or no-driving advice was delayed or unclear.

options.label: Unsafe
options.description: You waited for proof before acting or allowed David to drive.

stages.title: Why the plan changed
stages.intro: Three moments change what you need to do.

logic_moments.step: 1. Do not close the case early
logic_moments.signal: David calls it indigestion, but he also describes chest tightness
logic_moments.road_map: His label is only one possibility. Ask focused pain and warning-symptom questions.

logic_moments.signal: The pain started with exertion. It is central, heavy, severe, and not settling
logic_moments.road_map: Arm or jaw spread, sweating, nausea and breathlessness add to the concern. Call an ambulance now.

logic_moments.signal: David asks if he can drive himself
logic_moments.road_map: Explain that he could worsen suddenly. Keep him seated, continue checking him and prepare the handover.

retrieval_prompts: Why should David's word 'indigestion' not settle the diagnosis?
Which details mean you should call an ambulance now?
Why is driving unsafe while these symptoms continue?
stages.recovery_sentence: At first, indigestion was possible because the pain started after lunch. But the pain is spreading to your arm and jaw, you are sweaty and short of breath, and the pain is not settling. This could be a serious heart problem, so I am calling an ambulance now.

stages.title: Actual Run: the whole station
stages.intro: Say the parts marked You say, You ask the examiner, or You hand over. Read David's and the examiner's replies. Do each Action to take.
stages.completeness_note: This example covers the four station tasks. It is not a full treatment guide. Medication is outside this station example.
reading_time_plan: Read the four tasks and notice that you are in general practice.
Check the pain and warning symptoms. Act as soon as the dangerous pattern is clear.
While help is coming, keep David safe, ask for findings and prepare the handover.

learning_moments.signal: David says 'indigestion', but also describes chest tightness.
learning_moments.action: Do not agree too early. Check the pain and warning symptoms.
learning_moments.reason: A patient's first explanation can be wrong.

learning_moments.signal: Heavy central pain started with exertion and is not settling.
learning_moments.action: Move quickly through the remaining warning symptoms.
learning_moments.reason: The pattern is becoming more concerning.

learning_moments.signal: The pain spreads, and David is sweaty, sick and short of breath.
learning_moments.action: Call an ambulance now. Do not wait for clinic tests.
learning_moments.reason: You already have enough warning signs to act.

learning_moments.action: Explain the risk, keep him seated and prepare the handover.
learning_moments.reason: He could worsen suddenly on the way.

portable_station_spine.label: Open and confirm identity
portable_station_spine.candidate_job: Introduce yourself, confirm David's identity, and ask about the chest discomfort.

portable_station_spine.label: Ask about the pain
portable_station_spine.candidate_job: Ask when it started, what he was doing, where it is, what it feels like, how severe it is, where it spreads, and whether it is easing.

portable_station_spine.label: Check warning symptoms
portable_station_spine.candidate_job: Ask about sweating, nausea, shortness of breath, dizziness, and feeling faint.

portable_station_spine.label: Call an ambulance when warning signs are present
portable_station_spine.candidate_job: Explain the risk and call an ambulance as soon as the warning signs are clear.

portable_station_spine.label: Keep David seated and stop him driving
portable_station_spine.candidate_job: Explain why driving is unsafe and keep David seated while help is coming.

portable_station_spine.label: Ask the examiner for findings
portable_station_spine.candidate_job: Ask for vital signs, general appearance, and relevant cardiovascular and respiratory findings.

portable_station_spine.label: Ask about heart risk factors
portable_station_spine.candidate_job: Ask the remaining risk questions while the ambulance is coming.

portable_station_spine.label: Give a short handover
portable_station_spine.candidate_job: Include who David is, what happened, warning signs, risk factors, vital signs, your concern, and what you have done.

gold_run.speaker: Ushana
gold_run.spoken: Hello David, I'm Ushana, one of the doctors here. I understand you've had some chest discomfort. Before we talk about it, could I please confirm your full name and date of birth?
gold_run.road_map: Confirm who David is, then ask about the chest discomfort.

gold_run.speaker: David
gold_run.spoken: Doctor, I think it is just indigestion, but my chest feels really tight.
gold_run.road_map: David thinks it is indigestion, but chest tightness can be serious. Check before agreeing.

gold_run.speaker: Ushana
gold_run.spoken: I understand why you thought it was indigestion. Chest tightness can sometimes be serious, so I need to ask a few quick questions. When did it start, and what were you doing?
gold_run.road_map: Let David know you understand his idea. Explain why you need quick questions, then ask when the pain started.

gold_run.speaker: David
gold_run.spoken: It started about 45 minutes ago while I was carrying boxes.
gold_run.road_map: Pain that started with exertion is concerning.

gold_run.speaker: Ushana
gold_run.spoken: Can you point to where you feel it? Does it feel sharp, burning, heavy, or tight? How bad is it from zero to ten? Does it spread anywhere? Is it easing?
gold_run.road_map: Ask the key pain questions quickly.

gold_run.speaker: David
gold_run.spoken: It is in the centre, heavy and tight, about 8 out of 10. It goes into my left arm and jaw, and it is not settling.
gold_run.road_map: Heavy central pain that spreads and does not settle is a warning sign.

gold_run.speaker: Ushana
gold_run.spoken: Have you felt sweaty or sick? Have you felt short of breath, dizzy, or faint?
gold_run.road_map: Ask about symptoms that may occur with a heart emergency.

gold_run.speaker: David
gold_run.spoken: I feel sweaty and sick, and I am a bit short of breath. It is starting to scare me.
gold_run.road_map: David now has several warning signs. Call an ambulance.

gold_run.speaker: Ushana
gold_run.spoken: David, your symptoms may be caused by a serious heart problem. I cannot confirm the exact cause here, and it would not be safe to wait. I am calling an ambulance now while we keep checking you.
gold_run.road_map: You do not need the exact diagnosis to act. Explain the risk and call an ambulance.

gold_run.speaker: David
gold_run.spoken: An ambulance? Can I just drive myself? Is it really that serious?
gold_run.road_map: David wants to drive. Explain why that is unsafe.

gold_run.speaker: Ushana
gold_run.spoken: I understand that an ambulance feels like a big step. Your symptoms could get worse suddenly, so it is not safe for you to drive. The ambulance team can monitor and treat you on the way. Please stay seated while we wait.
gold_run.road_map: Explain the reason clearly. Keep your tone calm and firm.

gold_run.speaker: Non-spoken action
gold_run.instruction: Do this. Do not say it aloud.
gold_run.spoken: Call an ambulance now. Keep David seated and supervised. Check his vital signs. Prepare an ECG if available, but do not delay the ambulance.
gold_run.road_map: The ambulance is on the way. Keep checking David without delaying hospital care.

gold_run.speaker: Ushana
gold_run.spoken: Examiner, could I please have David's vital signs, general appearance, and relevant cardiovascular and respiratory examination findings? Is an ECG available in the clinic? Is a troponin result available during this consultation?
gold_run.road_map: Ask the examiner directly for the findings and tests you need.

gold_run.speaker: Examiner
gold_run.spoken:
David looks pale and sweaty.
Vital signs: heart rate 104, blood pressure 155/92, respiratory rate 22, oxygen saturation 95% on room air, temperature 36.8°C, and pain 8/10.
His chest is clear. There is no new murmur or calf swelling.
An ECG is available in the clinic. A troponin result is not available during this consultation.
gold_run.road_map: These findings support your concern. You already had enough warning signs to call the ambulance.

gold_run.speaker: Ushana
gold_run.spoken: While we wait for the ambulance, I need to ask a few more questions. Do you smoke? Do you have diabetes, high blood pressure, or high cholesterol? Has anyone in your close family had heart disease?
gold_run.road_map: Ask the remaining heart risk questions while the ambulance is coming.

gold_run.speaker: David
gold_run.spoken: I smoke. I have high blood pressure and high cholesterol, but not diabetes. My father had a heart attack.
gold_run.road_map: These risk factors support your concern and belong in the handover.

gold_run.speaker: Ushana
gold_run.spoken:
Ambulance team, this is David Nguyen, aged 58.
He has had 45 minutes of heavy central chest tightness that began while carrying boxes. It is 8 out of 10, spreads to his left arm and jaw, and has not settled. He is sweaty, nauseated, and short of breath.
He smokes and has high blood pressure and high cholesterol. His father had a heart attack.
His heart rate is 104, blood pressure 155/92, respiratory rate 22, and oxygen saturation 95% on room air.
I am concerned about a serious cardiac cause. He is resting and has been told not to drive.
gold_run.road_map: Give a short handover: who David is, what happened, warning signs, risk factors, vital signs, your concern, and what you have done.

traps.label: Believing the first explanation
traps.drift: The pain started after lunch, so it must be indigestion.
traps.reset: Treat indigestion as one possibility. Check the warning signs before deciding.

traps.label: Waiting for a test
traps.drift: I need an ECG or blood test before I call an ambulance.
traps.reset: David already has several warning signs. Call the ambulance first. Tests must not delay hospital care.

traps.label: Letting David drive
traps.drift: The hospital is close, so David can drive himself.
traps.reset: His symptoms could worsen suddenly. Keep him seated and use the ambulance.

stages.title: What if the details change?
stages.intro: Choose what you would do before reading the explanation.

transfer_drills.scenario: David calls the same heavy central pain and sweating 'trapped wind' instead of indigestion.

choices.label: Accept 'trapped wind' and watch him in the clinic.
Use the warning signs and arrange urgent hospital care now.
Wait for a clinic test before deciding.

transfer_drills.feedback_correct: Yes. David's explanation changed, but his warning signs did not.
transfer_drills.feedback_retry: Focus on what David is experiencing: heavy central pain with exertion and sweating. His name for it does not make it safe.

transfer_drills.scenario: The pain does not spread, but it remains central, heavy, severe, and brought on by exertion. David is also sweaty and short of breath.

choices.label: Continue urgent hospital care because the remaining warning signs are serious.
Treat it as less serious because the pain does not spread.
Take a long background history before deciding.

transfer_drills.feedback_correct: Yes. One missing feature does not cancel the other warning signs.
transfer_drills.feedback_retry: Look at all the information together. Central heavy pain with exertion, sweating, and shortness of breath is still serious.

transfer_drills.scenario: An early ECG is available, but David still has ongoing high-risk symptoms.
transfer_drills.prompt: When should you do the ECG?

choices.label: Do the ECG while the ambulance is coming. Do not delay hospital care.
Wait for the ECG before calling the ambulance.
If the ECG looks reassuring, let David drive.

transfer_drills.feedback_correct: Yes. The ECG can add information while the ambulance is coming.
transfer_drills.feedback_retry: Do the ECG while the ambulance is coming. It must not delay hospital care.

stages.title: Choose one part to practise
stages.intro: Choose one part. It will open immediately for a short spoken practice.

retry_options.label: Explain why it is urgent
retry_options.practice_task: Tell David why you are calling an ambulance without saying you know the diagnosis.
retry_options.hint: Say three things: I cannot confirm the cause here. Your symptoms may be from a serious heart problem. It is not safe to wait.
retry_options.model_line: Your symptoms may be caused by a serious heart problem. I cannot confirm the exact cause here, and it would not be safe to wait. I am calling an ambulance now.

retry_options.label: Explain why David must not drive
retry_options.practice_task: Answer David calmly when he asks to drive himself.
retry_options.hint: Let David know you understand that an ambulance feels like a big step. Explain that his symptoms could worsen suddenly. Then tell him not to drive.
retry_options.model_line: I understand that an ambulance feels like a big step. Your symptoms could get worse suddenly, so it is not safe for you to drive.

retry_options.label: Give the handover
retry_options.practice_task: Give a handover in under 45 seconds. Include who David is, what happened, warning signs, risk factors, vital signs, your concern, and what you have done.
retry_options.hint: Use this order: who he is, what happened, warning signs, vital signs, your concern, and your actions.
retry_options.model_line: David is 58 and has had 45 minutes of heavy central chest tightness that started with exertion. It spreads to his arm and jaw, and he is sweaty, nauseated, and short of breath. I am concerned about a serious cardiac cause and have called an ambulance.

stages.title: How ready do you feel now?
stages.intro: Choose the statement that fits best. The site will suggest what to practise next.

levels.label: I still need the Actual Run beside me.
I can spot the danger, but I may lose the order.
I can run the station without the example.
